channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government announces Rs22 per litre cut in petrol and diesel prices amid global oil trends
item.description: The government of Pakistan has announced a significant reduction in fuel prices, cutting both petrol and high-speed diesel (HSD) rates by Rs22 per litre each with immediate effect. This decision comes...

item.title: Community plans human circle protest to support Macquarie’s Big Splash water park
item.description: A community campaign to save the Big Splash water park at Macquarie in Canberra is calling on supporters to gather this Saturday at 3pm to form a human circle around the site. The Save Big Splash grou...

item.title: Rescue Operation in Laos Aims to Extract Five Villagers Trapped in Flooded Cave
item.description: A high-risk rescue operation is underway in central Laos to extract five villagers trapped in a remote, flooded cave. The men, who entered the cave searching for gold, have been stranded for over a we...

item.title: Allahabad High Court seeks Uttar Pradesh government's response on detention of former journalist linked to Noida workers’ protest
item.description: The Allahabad High Court has sought a formal response from the Uttar Pradesh government regarding a habeas corpus petition filed on behalf of former journalist Satyam Verma, who was detained in connec...

item.title: Tanzania launches digital system to monitor and fight pollution
item.description: The Prime Minister of Tanzania, Dr Mwigulu Nchemba, has officially launched the Tanzania Online Continuous Emission Monitoring System (TOCEMS), a new digital platform designed to strengthen the countr...

item.title: Segilola Resources commissions community water factory and 2.4km road project in Osun communities
item.description: Segilola Resources Operating Limited (SROL) has officially commissioned a series of Community Development Agreement (CDA) projects in Osun State, reinforcing its commitment to sustainable development,...

item.title: Families of Missing Sabungeros File Obstruction of Justice Complaint Against Lawyer of Atong Ang
item.description: Families of the missing sabungeros have taken action by filing an obstruction of justice complaint at the Department of Justice against the lawyer of businessman Atong Ang. According to Charlene Lasco...

item.title: President Ruto Plans Tax Relief for Low-Income Kenyans
item.description: President William Ruto has announced plans to exempt all Kenyans earning KSh30,000 and below from income tax. The announcement, made during the National Prayer Breakfast in Nairobi, aims to ease finan...

item.title: Blossoms Confirmed as Special Guests at Leeds Festival 2026
item.description: Blossoms, the Stockport-based indie band, have been confirmed as special guests for the northern leg of Reading & Leeds Festival 2026, performing at Leeds on Friday, 28th August. This marks their firs...

item.title: Police investigate North Melbourne rookie Lachy Dovaston over alleged egg-throwing incident
item.description: Police in Victoria are investigating an incident involving North Melbourne AFL draftee Lachy Dovaston following reports of late-night misconduct at a shopping centre car park in Rowville. According to...

item.title: Canada experiences technical recession after GDP stalls in early 2026
item.description: Statistics Canada has reported that Canada’s economy experienced a slight decline in the first quarter of 2026, meeting the technical definition of a recession after two consecutive quarters of negati...

item.title: PTI alleges arrest and expulsion of MNA Junaid Akbar during election campaign in Gilgit-Baltistan
item.description: According to reports from PTI and statements shared on social media platform X, PTI’s Khyber Pakhtunkhwa president and Member of National Assembly Junaid Akbar was allegedly stopped, detained, and lat...

item.title: Key national updates: NEET reform oversight, stock market fall, and security developments across India and abroad
item.description: The evening news update brings together several major developments from India and international affairs. The Centre informed the Supreme Court that Prime Minister Narendra Modi is personally monitorin...

item.title: Russell T Davies Explores Rising Homophobia and Neighbourhood Conflict in Channel 4 Drama 'Tip Toe'
item.description: Russell T Davies, acclaimed for the 2021 drama 'It’s a Sin', returns with a new Channel 4 series, 'Tip Toe', which examines the resurgence of homophobia through the tense feud between two Manchester n...

item.title: Tanzania invests over Sh1.3 billion to boost youth aquaculture projects in Mara region
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has announced an investment of more than Sh1.3 billion aimed at supporting youth-led fish farming initiatives in the Mara region. This funding is part of a broader strategy ...

item.title: Debate over Hugo Broos keeping omitted Bafana players at World Cup squad announcement event
item.description: Bafana Bafana head coach Hugo Broos has sparked widespread debate following his decision to include six players who were ultimately left out of the final 26-man squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup in th...

item.title: Key Philippine and global updates: Estrada graft case, Marcos policy statements, airfare outlook, and Iran-US ceasefire extension
item.description: The latest edition of Rappler’s The wRap for May 29, 2026 presents a roundup of significant political, legal, and international developments affecting the Philippines and the wider world. Senator Jing...

item.title: Shaheen Afridi backs Babar Azam to carry PSL form into Australia ODI series
item.description: Pakistan ODI captain Shaheen Shah Afridi has expressed optimism ahead of the upcoming three-match ODI series against Australia that senior batter Babar Azam will be able to carry forward his strong fo...

item.title: Sanwo-Olu Reflects on Seven Years in Office, Promises Continued Development in Lagos
item.description: Lagos State Governor, Babajide Sanwo-Olu, don assure citizens say him administration go continue to push infrastructural, housing, healthcare, and security projects as him mark seven years for office....

item.title: SIU targets alleged Tembisa Hospital corruption syndicate linked to R500m in irregular contracts
item.description: The Special Investigating Unit (SIU) has intensified its probe into large-scale corruption at Tembisa Hospital, focusing on alleged syndicate kingpin Stefan Joel Govindraju, who is believed to have be...
